get.summary:
ListArtifacts returns artifacts the caller has access to, with
optional filters.

parameters.name: typeFilter
parameters.description:
Optional type filter. If set, only artifacts of this type are
returned.

 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_OBJECT: Binary blob identified by a Storage Object.
 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_APP: Hosted static site deployed as a workload in the implementation's
runtime.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description:
Optional owner filter. If set, only artifacts owned by this user
are returned. Permitted values depend on implementation auth:
typically a non-admin caller may only set this to their own id.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description:
Optional run filter. If set, only artifacts produced during this
run are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Maximum number of artifacts to return. The server may cap the
value.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from a previous ListArtifactsResponse.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Implementation-defined order specification. Conventional format is
"<field> <asc|desc>", for example "created_at desc".

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description:
Optional session filter. If set, only artifacts attached to this
Workers session are returned.

post.summary:
CreateAppArtifact registers a new app artifact and triggers the
implementation's deploy pipeline. The returned Artifact may be in
DEPLOYMENT_STATE_PENDING; callers observe readiness via the
ARTIFACT_EVENT_TYPE_APP_DEPLOYED event on the Workers stream, or
by polling GetArtifact.
post.description:
The additional /v1/internal/artifacts/deploy-app binding mirrors
cr0n-a's worker-facing route shape so the chunk-14 Python worker's
deploy_app tool reuses cr0n's tool implementation verbatim
(modulo the body-shape remap in chunk 16).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateAppArtifactRequest is the request message for CreateAppArtifact.

post.summary:
CreateObjectArtifact registers a new object artifact and persists
its blob bytes as a Storage Object. The caller supplies bytes
inline; for large uploads, a future RPC will let callers register
an artifact around a pre-uploaded Storage Object.
post.description:
The additional /v1/internal/artifacts/deploy-object binding mirrors
cr0n-a's worker-facing route shape so the chunk-14 Python worker
(`cmd/nightshift-worker-claude/artifact_tools.py`) reuses cr0n's
tool implementations verbatim.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
CreateObjectArtifactRequest is the request message for
CreateObjectArtifact. The caller supplies blob bytes inline; the
implementation is responsible for persisting them as a Storage
Object.

This shape is a deliberate convenience for workers that produce small
artifacts (reports, images, JSON documents) in-memory. Large uploads
that benefit from presigned URLs should call Storage's
InitiateObjectUpload / FinalizeObjectUpload directly and then invoke
a future `RegisterObjectArtifact` variant; v1 of this spec does not
define that RPC.

get.summary:
GetArtifact reads an artifact by id. Permission is required — the
RPC fails with NOT_FOUND when the caller is neither owner nor
grantee nor (for `public=true` artifacts) any authenticated user.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

delete.summary:
DeleteArtifact removes an artifact, its permissions, its Storage
Objects, and (for apps) its hosted workload. Owner only.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

patch.summary:
UpdateArtifact modifies metadata, visibility, and/or bytes of an
existing artifact. Owner or editor role required; public/private
toggles are owner-only.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

get.summary:
ListArtifactPermissions returns all non-owner grants on an
artifact. Any role suffices.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

post.summary:
ShareArtifact grants or re-grants a permission on an artifact.
Owner only.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

delete.summary: RevokeArtifactShare removes a permission grant. Owner only.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: User whose grant should be removed. No-op if no grant exists.

get.summary:
GetArtifactDownloadURL returns a presigned URL the caller can GET
to fetch the blob bytes of an object artifact. Any role (viewer,
editor, owner) or public access suffices.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier. Must refer to an object artifact.

get.summary:
GetArtifactPreviewURL returns a presigned URL the caller can GET
to fetch the companion HTML preview of an object artifact. Fails
with NOT_FOUND when the artifact has no preview.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description:
Target artifact identifier. Must refer to an object artifact with
`has_preview` = true; otherwise the RPC fails with NOT_FOUND.

post.summary:
CreateAppArtifact registers a new app artifact and triggers the
implementation's deploy pipeline. The returned Artifact may be in
DEPLOYMENT_STATE_PENDING; callers observe readiness via the
ARTIFACT_EVENT_TYPE_APP_DEPLOYED event on the Workers stream, or
by polling GetArtifact.
post.description:
The additional /v1/internal/artifacts/deploy-app binding mirrors
cr0n-a's worker-facing route shape so the chunk-14 Python worker's
deploy_app tool reuses cr0n's tool implementation verbatim
(modulo the body-shape remap in chunk 16).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateAppArtifactRequest is the request message for CreateAppArtifact.

post.summary:
CreateObjectArtifact registers a new object artifact and persists
its blob bytes as a Storage Object. The caller supplies bytes
inline; for large uploads, a future RPC will let callers register
an artifact around a pre-uploaded Storage Object.
post.description:
The additional /v1/internal/artifacts/deploy-object binding mirrors
cr0n-a's worker-facing route shape so the chunk-14 Python worker
(`cmd/nightshift-worker-claude/artifact_tools.py`) reuses cr0n's
tool implementations verbatim.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
CreateObjectArtifactRequest is the request message for
CreateObjectArtifact. The caller supplies blob bytes inline; the
implementation is responsible for persisting them as a Storage
Object.

This shape is a deliberate convenience for workers that produce small
artifacts (reports, images, JSON documents) in-memory. Large uploads
that benefit from presigned URLs should call Storage's
InitiateObjectUpload / FinalizeObjectUpload directly and then invoke
a future `RegisterObjectArtifact` variant; v1 of this spec does not
define that RPC.

get.summary:
ListArtifacts returns artifacts the caller has access to, with
optional filters.

parameters.name: typeFilter
parameters.description:
Optional type filter. If set, only artifacts of this type are
returned.

 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_OBJECT: Binary blob identified by a Storage Object.
 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_APP: Hosted static site deployed as a workload in the implementation's
runtime.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description:
Optional owner filter. If set, only artifacts owned by this user
are returned. Permitted values depend on implementation auth:
typically a non-admin caller may only set this to their own id.

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description:
Optional run filter. If set, only artifacts produced during this
run are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Maximum number of artifacts to return. The server may cap the
value.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from a previous ListArtifactsResponse.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Implementation-defined order specification. Conventional format is
"<field> <asc|desc>", for example "created_at desc".

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description:
Optional session filter. If set, only artifacts attached to this
Workers session are returned.

post.summary:
ShareArtifact grants or re-grants a permission on an artifact.
Owner only.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

put.summary:
UpdateArtifact modifies metadata, visibility, and/or bytes of an
existing artifact. Owner or editor role required; public/private
toggles are owner-only.

parameters.name: artifactId
parameters.description: Target artifact identifier.

role.description: Role to grant. ARTIFACT_ROLE_OWNER is rejected in v1.

ArtifactsShareArtifactBody.description:
ShareArtifactRequest is the request message for ShareArtifact.

Only the artifact owner may call ShareArtifact. Sharing is
idempotent at the (artifact_id, user_id) pair: a repeated call with
a new role replaces the existing grant.

public.description:
Optional new visibility. For app artifacts the implementation
handles the ingress toggle; for object artifacts this only changes
access enforcement, not the underlying Storage Object.

htmlContent.description: App-only: replacement HTML content. Ignored for objects.

contentType.description:
Object-only: MIME type of `content_bytes`. Required whenever
`content_bytes` is non-empty.

previewHtml.description:
Object-only: replacement companion preview HTML. Empty bytes
explicitly clears any prior preview.

setPreviewHtml.description:
Whether `preview_html` is being set (true) or left alone (false).
Needed to distinguish "clear the preview" (set=true, bytes empty)
from "leave the preview" (set=false).

ArtifactsUpdateArtifactBody.description:
UpdateArtifactRequest is the request message for UpdateArtifact.

Any field left unset is preserved. Callers supply `name`,
`description`, and/or `public` to change metadata; `content` (apps)
or `content_bytes` + `content_type` (objects) to rewrite the bytes.
`preview_html` follows `content_bytes` and may be set independently
for objects to ship a new preview without rewriting the binary.

id.description: Server-assigned identifier. Stable for the lifetime of the artifact.

type.description:
Whether this artifact is an object (blob) or an app (hosted static
site).

name.description:
Human-readable display name. For object artifacts this is typically
the filename.

description.description: Optional short description supplied by the creator.

ownerId.description:
Identifier of the user who created the artifact. Treated opaquely;
implementations bind it to their auth identity model.

runId.description:
Identifier of the run during which this artifact was produced, or
empty if the artifact was created outside a run (for example via a
direct user upload).

public.description:
When false (default) access is gated by ownership + permission
grants. When true, access is unauthenticated.

objectBucket.description:
Object-only: the Storage Object holding the blob bytes. Empty for
app artifacts.

contentType.description: Object-only: MIME type of the blob bytes. Empty for app artifacts.

sizeBytes.description:
Object-only: size of the blob in bytes, as reported by Storage
after finalization. Zero for app artifacts.

hasPreview.description:
Object-only: whether a companion preview HTML was uploaded to the
implementation's preview key convention for this artifact. When
true, GetArtifactPreviewURL will return a URL.

appUrl.description:
App-only: URL at which the hosted workload serves content. For
private apps this is the implementation's permission-gated proxy
URL; for public apps this is the public ingress URL. Empty for
object artifacts.

appStatus.description:
App-only: deployment lifecycle state. DEPLOYMENT_STATE_UNSPECIFIED
for object artifacts.

appStatusDetail.description:
App-only: optional free-form reason string accompanying FAILED
state.

createdAt.description: Time the artifact record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Time the artifact record was most recently updated.

sessionId.description:
Workers session this artifact is attached to, or empty. Typically
set on user-uploaded artifacts and left empty on worker-produced
artifacts (which are scoped via run_id).

v1Artifact.description:
Artifact is the metadata record for an agent-produced or user-uploaded
output. The blob bytes (for objects) and the hosted workload (for
apps) live elsewhere; this message describes what, who, and where.

artifactId.description: The artifact this grant applies to.

userId.description: The user the grant is made to.

role.description:
The role granted. The implicit owner grant is not represented as a
row and will not be returned by ListArtifactPermissions.

v1ArtifactPermission.description:
ArtifactPermission is a single (artifact_id, user_id, role) grant. At
most one permission per (artifact_id, user_id) pair exists at any
time; re-granting a role replaces the prior grant.

v1ArtifactRole.description:
ArtifactRole is the permission level granted to a user on an artifact.

 - ARTIFACT_ROLE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - ARTIFACT_ROLE_VIEWER: Read-only access. May download / view / preview.
 - ARTIFACT_ROLE_EDITOR: Read + update access. May call UpdateArtifact. May not share,
delete, or toggle public/private.
 - ARTIFACT_ROLE_OWNER: Full control. Implicit on the creator and may not be granted via
ShareArtifact in v1.

v1ArtifactType.description:
ArtifactType distinguishes object artifacts (blob content served via
download URL) from app artifacts (static sites served as a hosted
workload).

 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_OBJECT: Binary blob identified by a Storage Object.
 - ARTIFACT_TYPE_APP: Hosted static site deployed as a workload in the implementation's
runtime.

htmlContent.description:
Self-contained HTML content. Implementations serve this as the
entry document of the deployed app. CSS and JS must be inlined or
referenced by absolute URL; relative asset references are not
supported in v1.

runId.description: Run during which this artifact was produced, or empty.

public.description:
Initial visibility. Defaults to false (private). Public apps are
served at the implementation's public endpoint without an
authentication check.

v1CreateAppArtifactRequest.description: CreateAppArtifactRequest is the request message for CreateAppArtifact.

v1CreateAppArtifactResponse.description:
CreateAppArtifactResponse is the response message for
CreateAppArtifact. The returned Artifact may be in
DEPLOYMENT_STATE_PENDING — the caller should subscribe to Workers
streaming events for ARTIFACT_EVENT_TYPE_APP_DEPLOYED, or poll
GetArtifact, to observe readiness.

name.description:
Display name / filename for the artifact (for example
"Q1-summary.pdf"). Extensions are part of the name; Storage does
not infer MIME types from them.

content.description:
The blob bytes. The implementation stores them as a Storage Object
under an implementation-defined (bucket, key) convention.

ownerId.description:
Creator user id. Implementations may derive this from auth context
and ignore the field; worker callers supply it explicitly.

runId.description: Run during which this artifact was produced, or empty.

previewHtml.description:
Optional companion HTML rendering of the same content. When
provided, implementations upload it to their preview-key convention
so GetArtifactPreviewURL returns a usable URL. Used by agent
report generators (PDF/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX) to ship an inline-viewable
preview alongside the native binary format.

idempotencyKey.description:
Optional idempotency key. A repeat request with the same key from
the same caller within the implementation-defined window returns
the original response.

sessionId.description:
Optional Workers session this artifact is attached to. Worker
callers typically set run_id instead.

v1CreateObjectArtifactRequest.description:
CreateObjectArtifactRequest is the request message for
CreateObjectArtifact. The caller supplies blob bytes inline; the
implementation is responsible for persisting them as a Storage
Object.

This shape is a deliberate convenience for workers that produce small
artifacts (reports, images, JSON documents) in-memory. Large uploads
that benefit from presigned URLs should call Storage's
InitiateObjectUpload / FinalizeObjectUpload directly and then invoke
a future `RegisterObjectArtifact` variant; v1 of this spec does not
define that RPC.

v1CreateObjectArtifactResponse.description:
CreateObjectArtifactResponse is the response message for
CreateObjectArtifact.

v1DeleteArtifactResponse.description:
DeleteArtifactResponse is the response message for DeleteArtifact. It
is intentionally empty.

v1DeploymentState.description:
DeploymentState is the lifecycle of an app artifact's hosted workload.

 - DEPLOYMENT_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this
for app artifacts once the initial deploy has started.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STATE_PENDING: The deploy has been accepted and the workload is being created.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STATE_READY: The workload is reachable at `app_url`.
 - DEPLOYMENT_STATE_FAILED: The most recent deploy or update failed. `app_status_detail` carries
an implementation-specific reason.

downloadUrl.description: Presigned URL the caller can GET to fetch the blob bytes.

v1GetArtifactDownloadURLResponse.description:
GetArtifactDownloadURLResponse is the response message for
GetArtifactDownloadURL.

previewUrl.description:
Presigned URL the caller can GET to fetch the companion HTML
preview.

v1GetArtifactPreviewURLResponse.description:
GetArtifactPreviewURLResponse is the response message for
GetArtifactPreviewURL.

v1GetArtifactResponse.description: GetArtifactResponse is the response message for GetArtifact.

permissions.description: All non-owner permissions currently granted on the artifact.

v1ListArtifactPermissionsResponse.description:
ListArtifactPermissionsResponse is the response message for
ListArtifactPermissions.

nextPageToken.description: Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further results.

v1ListArtifactsResponse.description: ListArtifactsResponse is the response message for ListArtifacts.

v1RevokeArtifactShareResponse.description:
RevokeArtifactShareResponse is the response message for
RevokeArtifactShare. It is intentionally empty.

v1ShareArtifactResponse.description: ShareArtifactResponse is the response message for ShareArtifact.

v1UpdateArtifactResponse.description: UpdateArtifactResponse is the response message for UpdateArtifact.
